    18→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (factorial residues modulo primes, asymptotic density).

    26→

    28→
    29→- [x] `factorialResidues` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`) at lines 43–44.
    30→- [x] No `noncomputable` needed — `Finset.Icc`, `.image`, `Nat.factorial`, and `%` are all computable.
    31→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.

    33→- N/A: No custom types, no local notation needed, no sanity-check attributes (optional).
    34→
    35→## 6. Theorem Statements
    36→
    37→- [x] Uses `theorem` keyword.
    38→- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_478` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    39→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 11]` (line 57).
    40→- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 48–56).
    41→- [x] Docstring references source paper: `[ErGr80, p.96]`.
    42→
    43→### `answer()` usage
    44→
    45→The problem asks "Is it true that |A_p| ~ (1 − 1/e) · p?" — a yes/no question → **Case 1**.
    46→
    47→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
    48→- [x] RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: the ratio `|A_p| / ((1 − e⁻¹) · p)` tends to 1 as `p → ∞` through primes.
    49→- [x] Quantifiers are on the RHS (after `↔`), not as theorem parameters.
    50→
    51→## 7. Category Attribute
    52→
    53→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
    54→- [x] Value is `research open` — a valid category.
    55→- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.
    56→
    57→## 8. AMS Attribute
    58→
    59→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
    60→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11` (Number theory).
    61→- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single number — trivially satisfied).
    62→- [x] No duplicates.
    63→- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
    64→
    65→## 9. Proof Bodies
    66→
    67→- [x] Open conjecture uses `by sorry` (multi-line form: `:= by\n  sorry` at lines 63–64, acceptable per checklist).
    68→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    69→- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
    70→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    71→
    72→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    73→
    74→- N/A: No variants present; the file addresses a single conjecture.
    75→
    76→## 11. Code Quality
    77→
    78→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    79→- [x] Unicode math symbols used (`ℕ`, `ℝ`).
    80→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    81→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    82→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    83→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    84→
    85→## 12. Build Verification
    86→
    87→- [ ] `lake build` not verified in this review.
    88→
    89→## Summary
    90→
    91→**Result: PASS** (all checkable items pass).
    92→
    93→The formalization is clean and mathematically faithful. The definition `factorialResidues` correctly captures A_p = { k! mod p : 1 ≤ k < p } using `Finset.Icc 1 (p - 1)` with `.image`. The asymptotic equivalence |A_p| ~ (1 − 1/e) · p is correctly expressed as Tendsto of the ratio to 1, filtered through `atTop ⊓ Filter.principal {p | Nat.Prime p}` to restrict to primes tending to infinity.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/478.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (line 19).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 478`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/478`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (factorial residues modulo primes, asymptotic density).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[ErGr80]` and `[GSSV24]` with author names and years.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos478 ... end Erdos478`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos478`).
- [x] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block (lines 37–39: `open Filter`, `open scoped Topology Real`).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `factorialResidues` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`) at lines 43–44.
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed — `Finset.Icc`, `.image`, `Nat.factorial`, and `%` are all computable.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Function name `factorialResidues` uses `lowerCamelCase`.
- N/A: No custom types, no local notation needed, no sanity-check attributes (optional).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_478` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 11]` (line 57).
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 48–56).
- [x] Docstring references source paper: `[ErGr80, p.96]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks "Is it true that |A_p| ~ (1 − 1/e) · p?" — a yes/no question → **Case 1**.

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
- [x] RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: the ratio `|A_p| / ((1 − e⁻¹) · p)` tends to 1 as `p → ∞` through primes.
- [x] Quantifiers are on the RHS (after `↔`), not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research open` — a valid category.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11` (Number theory).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single number — trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicates.
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjecture uses `by sorry` (multi-line form: `:= by\n  sorry` at lines 63–64, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present; the file addresses a single conjecture.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used (`ℕ`, `ℝ`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not verified in this review.

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (all checkable items pass).

The formalization is clean and mathematically faithful. The definition `factorialResidues` correctly captures A_p = { k! mod p : 1 ≤ k < p } using `Finset.Icc 1 (p - 1)` with `.image`. The asymptotic equivalence |A_p| ~ (1 − 1/e) · p is correctly expressed as Tendsto of the ratio to 1, filtered through `atTop ⊓ Filter.principal {p | Nat.Prime p}` to restrict to primes tending to infinity.

content.text: The review **passed** — all checkable items pass. No changes needed.
